summary: Stunning acoustic‑heavy May 1 1970 show captured on a soundboard with generally good fidelity despite some low‑end muddiness and occasional tape quirks.
review: The performance is hailed as one of the best early‑70 acoustic sets, with legendary renditions of “Me & My Uncle,” “Mama Tried,” “Deep Elem Blues” and a blistering “Speedway Boogie.”  The SBD source is mostly clear and well‑balanced, though reviewers note occasional oversaturation below 125 Hz, a few mic‑noise spots and minor tape‑speed glitches.  Overall the music outweighs the technical flaws, making this a prized collector’s item.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (≈30) are 4‑5 stars and praise the musical performance; recording issues are mentioned but not enough to outweigh the strong show quality, yielding a high combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp SBD mix with excellent stereo separation. Some reviewers note harsh oversaturation in low frequencies on one source, occasional mic‑noise on “I Know You Rider,” a cardboard‑box beat on “Cumberland Blues,” and minor tape‑speed/pitch corrections needed on a few tracks.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The acoustic first set flows smoothly with unique guest harmonies (NRPS) and rare arrangements; the electric second set keeps the energy high, though a few cuts in “The Other One” and “Love Light” are noted.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for his guitar tone, inventive electric lead on an acoustic song, and vocal embellishments on “Mama Tried”/“Me & My Uncle.”
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned loading gear after the show and early rehearsal of “I Know You Rider”; no specific performance critique.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Hart referenced in a personal anecdote about loading wagons; no direct drumming evaluation.
